name: Yudhishthira
core_quality: Absolute dharma — cannot lie even at cost of everything he loves
flaw: Passivity — his righteousness becomes inability to act when action is needed
story_arc_type: The righteous leader who suffers because the world is not righteous
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For heroes who hold a kingdom together through moral authority — not strength
villain_use: Villains who corrupt the definition of dharma to serve themselves
avoid: Do not use for active combat heroes — Yudhishthira does not fight first

name: Bhima
core_quality: Unstoppable physical power + fierce loyal love + inability to tolerate injustice
flaw: Rage that can become uncontrolled / underestimated intelligence
story_arc_type: The protector who breaks when those he loves are harmed
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For heroes with berserker-adjacent powers — Bihar (Vajramukha) directly references
villain_use: Villains who use Bhima's rage quality but without his love — pure destruction
avoid: Do not make Bhima-parallels cold or calculating — his rage comes from love

name: Arjuna
core_quality: Perfect skill + doubt at the critical moment → Bhagavad Gita
flaw: Pride in skill / paralysis when duty conflicts with love
weapon_or_skill: Gandiva bow + divine weapons from all gods
story_arc_type: The doubt-then-act hero — the Bhagavad Gita arc of recognising duty
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For skilled heroes who hesitate at the crucial moment before transcending doubt
villain_use: Villains who have Arjuna's skill without his Gita — pure technical mastery without soul
avoid: Do not make the Arjuna parallel obvious — let it emerge through the doubt scene

name: Nakula
core_quality: Beauty + loyalty + mastery of horses / medicine
flaw: Often overlooked — written out of most adaptations
story_arc_type: The overlooked twin who is quietly essential
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For supporting characters and secondary heroes who enable the main hero
villain_use: Villains of charm — who use beauty and loyalty to serve wrong sides
avoid: Rarely the main hero — better as essential supporting character

name: Sahadeva
core_quality: Knowledge of past present and future / cursed to silence / quiet wisdom
flaw: Cannot share what he knows — the burden of prophecy
story_arc_type: The one who knows and cannot say — the grief of foresight
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For guru characters and for heroes who know more than they can act on
villain_use: Villains who weaponise prophecy — who use knowledge of fate to control others
avoid: Do not give Sahadeva-parallels easy ways to share their knowledge — the constraint IS the character

name: Draupadi
core_quality: Fierce self-respect + justice that refuses to be silenced + dignity under humiliation
flaw: Nothing — she is the moral compass of the Mahabharata
weapon_or_skill: Fire herself (born from fire) / her voice / her faith in Krishna
story_arc_type: The one who demands justice even when everyone else has accepted defeat
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For female heroes who are wronged and refuse to accept it — Tamil Nadu (Kurinjiselvi) and West Bengal (Sundarvani)
villain_use: Villains who corrupt female power — who use Draupadi's fire to burn rather than illuminate
avoid: Never make the Draupadi parallel passive — she is always the one demanding accountability

name: Karna
role: Tragic hero of the other side
core_quality: Unmatched generosity + skill + loyalty that is placed in the wrong alliance
flaw: Born on the wrong side — unable to change alliance even when he knows it is wrong
weapon_or_skill: Vijaya bow + kavach-kundala (divine armour and earrings)
story_arc_type: The hero whose nobility is wasted because of where he was born
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: The villain who is actually more heroic than the heroes — the tragic figure who deserved better
villain_use: For heroes who are tragically loyal to a cause that fails them — Arunachal Pradesh (Udayagiri / Karna connection)
avoid: The most important villain template — use whenever you need the audience to feel sorry for the villain

name: Abhimanyu
core_quality: Courage beyond his years + entering the chakravyuha knowing he cannot escape
flaw: Youth — he knew how to enter but not how to exit
story_arc_type: The young hero who knows only half the knowledge needed — and dies for it
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For stories where the hero's incomplete knowledge leads to their undoing — and others must complete the lesson
villain_use: Villains who trap heroes in situations the heroes know how to enter but not exit
avoid: Use carefully — the Abhimanyu parallel always ends in sacrifice

name: Bheeshma
core_quality: Absolute self-discipline + wisdom + the terrifying cost of an unbreakable vow
flaw: His vow of celibacy and loyalty to Hastinapura blinds him to choosing dharma over duty
weapon_or_skill: Amba's arrow (the only thing that can kill him) / Shantanu's blessing to choose his own death
story_arc_type: The elder who chose a vow over his own humanity — and watched the world burn for it
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: The ancestral figure who is wise but bound — cannot act as they know they should
villain_use: Guru characters who cannot fully help because of a prior commitment or vow
avoid: Never make a Bheeshma-parallel villainous — they are tragic but they are fundamentally honourable

name: Vidura
core_quality: Speaking truth to power even when it costs everything — quiet moral courage
flaw: Powerlessness — he always knew and could rarely act
story_arc_type: The one in the court who always speaks the right thing and is always ignored until too late
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For secondary characters who see what is coming — the advisor the hero doesn't listen to early enough
villain_use: Villains who have Vidura's intelligence but use it to further injustice rather than oppose it
avoid: The Vidura-parallel must never be the main hero — they are always adjacent to power

name: Kunti
core_quality: Sacrifice that is never acknowledged — giving up everything for her sons' future
flaw: A secret she cannot share (Karna) — love that cannot be expressed for strategic reasons
weapon_or_skill: Surya mantra (sun god's boon) / her suffering
story_arc_type: The mother who holds the family together through impossible sacrifice
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For elder female characters — maternal mentors — the ones whose sacrifice drives the hero
villain_use: Villains who are Kunti's shadow — who sacrifice others for their own children's gain
avoid: The Kunti-parallel always has a secret — a truth they cannot tell

name: Gandhari
core_quality: Blindfolded devotion — choosing to experience her husband's world
flaw: Her blind love for her children leads her to excuse what she should have stopped
weapon_or_skill: Bandaged eyes that become literal blindness / her curse on Krishna
story_arc_type: The one whose love becomes enabling — who could have stopped the tragedy and chose not to
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For villains' supporters and enablers — those who love wrongly
villain_use: Rare hero use — only for heroes who must face the consequences of loving the wrong cause
avoid: The Gandhari parallel always faces the consequences of their enabling

name: Duryodhana
core_quality: Absolute conviction of his own righteousness — not evil but deeply convinced he is right
flaw: Pride + inability to admit mistake + the point of no return he keeps choosing
story_arc_type: The villain who sees himself as the hero of his own story — and is not entirely wrong
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: Primary villain template — every major Hulmane-The-Saga villain should have this quality of self-righteous conviction
villain_use: Central — this IS the villain template
avoid: Never make Duryodhana purely cruel — his tragedy is that he had real claims and real friendship

name: Shakuni
core_quality: Genius-level intelligence weaponised by real historical grievance
flaw: The grievance that consumed the person — he is entirely right about the wrong but entirely wrong about the response
weapon_or_skill: Dice / words / manipulation of others' desires
story_arc_type: The manipulator whose brilliance destroys more than it saves
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For secondary villains who work through others — the advisor who corrupts
villain_use: Primary villain template for intelligent manipulators
avoid: Never make Shakuni simply evil — his hatred of Hastinapura is justified. The injustice is real. The method is wrong.

name: Ashwatthama
role: Son of Drona / immortal bearer of a wound
core_quality: Grief that broke his moral compass — every atrocity he commits comes from love of his father
flaw: The grief that became revenge that became something he can never take back
weapon_or_skill: Brahmastra (the most destructive weapon) / his immortal forehead gem
story_arc_type: The villain who went too far and cannot come back — and knows it
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For villains in their final stage — when they have committed something they cannot undo
villain_use: The immortal villain who wanders the world carrying the weight of what they did — the Void Maw echoes this
avoid: Most tragic villain — never use Ashwatthama as a character who doesn't know what they did

name: Jayadratha
core_quality: Opportunism — taking advantage when the real warriors are away
flaw: Cowardice disguised as strategy — he never fights fairly
weapon_or_skill: Boon from Shiva (held Pandavas for one day)
story_arc_type: The minor villain who gets a moment of power and uses it cruelly
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For side villains and local antagonists — the ones who exploit a gap in the hero's defences
avoid: Never give a Jayadratha-parallel a heroic death — their arc ends in exposure

name: Dushasana
core_quality: Blind loyalty that requires no thought — following without question
flaw: No capacity for independent moral thought — complete capture by loyalty
story_arc_type: The enforcer — the one who does what the main villain cannot do themselves
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For villain lieutenants — the muscle behind the plan
avoid: Never make Dushasana interesting — his role is to be the act the audience cannot forgive

name: Drona
core_quality: Supreme mastery of the warrior arts — teacher of both Pandavas and Kauravas
flaw: Loyalty to the hand that feeds / turning his student into a weapon against dharma / Ekalavya
story_arc_type: The guru who failed his dharma — who taught power without wisdom
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For guru characters who have betrayed the dharmic duty of teaching — the warning of what a guru must not become
villain_use: Villains who pervert the student-teacher relationship — who teach for control not liberation
avoid: The Drona-parallel always has an Ekalavya — someone they denied what they deserved

name: Krishna
core_quality: Infinite wisdom + the willingness to let others choose freely + the paradox of divine intervention
flaw: He appears to not act while orchestrating everything — the divine cannot be fully understood
weapon_or_skill: Sudarshana Chakra + flute + the Bhagavad Gita itself
story_arc_type: The guide who gives perfect counsel and lets the hero fail if they don't hear it
how_to_use_in_hulmane-the-saga: For the cosmic force behind the story — the unseen hand — the Void Maw arc is Krishna-in-reverse
villain_use: Not a villain template — but Kali Yuga versions of Krishna appear as figures who give the right counsel for wrong ends
avoid: Never make Krishna a character who CAN be fully understood — he must remain partly beyond
